I now have comprehensive evidence to trace the full lifecycle. Here is the complete answer:

```json
{
  "workflow_name": "HTTP Request Lifecycle (Route matched → handler → response)",
  "flow": [
    "1. Client sends HTTP request → Node.js http.Server emits 'request' event → calls preRouting(req, res)",
    "2. preRouting (fastify.js:wrapRouting, line 795-812) runs optional rewriteUrl, then calls router.routing(req, res) which is find-my-way's router.lookup",
    "3. router.lookup (find-my-way) matches method+URL against registered routes. On match: calls routeHandler(req, res, params, context, query); on no-match: calls defaultRoute (fastify.js line 537-546) → fourOhFour.router.lookup → 404",
    "4. routeHandler (lib/route.js, line 457-583) creates Request object via new context.Request(), Reply object via new context.Reply(), generates request ID, creates child logger, logs incoming request, sets up response listeners (setupResponseListeners in lib/reply.js line 953-995), sets up handler timeout if configured, tracks keep-alive sockets for graceful shutdown",
    "5. routeHandler begins lifecycle: calls onRequestHookRunner (lib/hooks.js) if context.onRequest !== null, else directly calls runPreParsing (lib/route.js line 644-659)",
    "6. runPreParsing: runs preParsingHookRunner (lib/hooks.js) if context.preParsing !== null, allowing user hooks to modify the request payload stream, then calls handleRequest (lib/handle-request.js line 21-72)",
    "7. handleRequest: determines if method is bodyless (GET/HEAD/TRACE) or has body. For bodyless: calls handler() immediately. For methods with body: checks Content-Type header, runs content-type parser (request[kRouteContext].contentTypeParser.run()), then calls handler()",
    "8. handler (lib/handle-request.js line 26-33): runs preValidationHookRunner (lib/hooks.js) if context.preValidation !== null, then calls preValidationCallback",
    "9. preValidationCallback (lib/handle-request.js line 35-54): calls validateSchema() for schema validation of headers/params/querystring/body, then calls validationCompleted",
    "10. validationCompleted (lib/handle-request.js line 56-68): If validation error and attachValidation is false → reply.send(validationErr). Otherwise runs preHandlerHookRunner (lib/hooks.js) if context.preHandler !== null, else calls preHandlerCallback",
    "11. preHandlerCallback (lib/handle-request.js line 134-154) → preHandlerCallbackInner (line 156-199): executes context.handler(request, reply). If handler returns a thenable → wrapThenable. If handler returns non-undefined value → reply.send(result). If handler throws → reply[kReplyIsError]=true, reply.send(err)",
    "12. Reply.prototype.send (lib/reply.js line 150-233): determines payload type. If Error → onErrorHook. If undefined → onSendHook. If stream/Response/Buffer/string → onSendHook. If object → preSerializationHook → serialization → onSendHook",
    "13. preSerializationHook (lib/reply.js line 544-549): runs preSerializationHookRunner. Serializes payload via reply serializer or JSON.stringify or schema serializer, then calls onSendHook",
    "14. onSendHook (lib/reply.js line 551-563): runs onSendHookRunner. User onSend hooks can modify payload, then calls wrapOnSendEnd → onSendEnd",
    "15. onSendEnd (lib/reply.js line 585-691): writes status code + headers via safeWriteHead, then sends payload: string/Buffer via res.end(), streams via payload.pipe(res), web streams via sendWebStream. Handles trailers via sendTrailer/sendStreamTrailer",
    "16. Response finishes → setupResponseListeners callback fires (lib/reply.js line 953-995): records end time, cleans up timeout/signal resources, runs onResponseHookRunner if context.onResponse !== null, then logs request completion via requestCompleted"
  ],
  "state_transitions": [
    "NOT_STARTED → STARTED: fastify() called, Avvio boot initializes plugins, routes registered via router.on()",
    "STARTED → LISTENING: .listen() called, Node.js server starts accepting connections, onListen hooks run",
    "LISTENING → REQUEST_RECEIVED: Incoming HTTP request, Node.js emits 'request' event, preRouting callback invoked",
    "REQUEST_RECEIVED → ROUTING: wrapRouting (fastify.js:795) calls router.routing() (find-my-way lookup)",
    "ROUTING → LOAD_SHEDDING (if closing): Server is closing → 503 Service Unavailable returned immediately (lib/route.js:476-487)",
    "ROUTING → REQUEST_READY: Route matched, routeHandler creates Request object, Reply object, child logger (lib/route.js:457-583)",
    "REQUEST_READY → ON_REQUEST_HOOKS: context.onRequest hooks run via onRequestHookRunner (lib/hooks.js:276)",
    "ON_REQUEST_HOOKS → PRE_PARSING_HOOKS: runPreParsing (lib/route.js:644-659) runs preParsingHookRunner, allows payload stream modification",
    "PRE_PARSING_HOOKS → BODY_PARSING: handleRequest (lib/handle-request.js:21-72) determines bodyless vs body-with methods, runs contentTypeParser.run() for body parsing",
    "BODY_PARSING → PRE_VALIDATION_HOOKS: handler() (lib/handle-request.js:26-33) runs preValidationHookRunner",
    "PRE_VALIDATION_HOOKS → VALIDATION: preValidationCallback (lib/handle-request.js:35-54) calls validateSchema() for schema validation of headers/params/querystring/body",
    "VALIDATION → PRE_HANDLER_HOOKS: validationCompleted (lib/handle-request.js:56-68) runs preHandlerHookRunner if context.preHandler !== null",
    "PRE_HANDLER_HOOKS → HANDLER_EXECUTION: preHandlerCallbackInner (lib/handle-request.js:156-199) executes context.handler(request, reply)",
    "HANDLER_EXECUTION → PRE_SERIALIZATION_HOOKS: reply.send(payload) (lib/reply.js:150-233) routes to preSerializationHook for object payloads",
    "PRE_SERIALIZATION_HOOKS → SERIALIZATION: preSerializationHookEnd (lib/reply.js:571-591) serializes payload via reply serializer or JSON.stringify or schema-compiled serializer",
    "SERIALIZATION → ON_SEND_HOOKS: onSendHook (lib/reply.js:551-563) runs onSendHookRunner, allows user hooks to modify final payload",
    "ON_SEND_HOOKS → WRITE_RESPONSE: onSendEnd (lib/reply.js:585-691) writes headers via safeWriteHead, then writes payload body (stream, Buffer, or string) to Node.js response",
    "WRITE_RESPONSE → ON_RESPONSE_HOOKS: Response 'finish' event fires, setupResponseListeners callback runs onResponseHookRunner (lib/reply.js:953-995)",
    "ON_RESPONSE_HOOKS → COMPLETED: onResponseCallback logs request completed via requestCompleted (lib/reply.js:997-999)"
  ],
  "external_boundaries": [
    "Node.js http.Server / https.Server / http2.Server — receives raw TCP connections, emits 'request' event on each HTTP request (lib/server.js:getServerInstance)",
    "find-my-way router — 3rd-party radix-tree router that matches HTTP method+URL to route handlers. Exposed via buildRouting (lib/route.js:77) as router.lookup.bind(router)",
    "Content-Type parsers (lib/content-type-parser.js) — pluggable system for parsing request bodies based on Content-Type header, triggered in handleRequest (lib/handle-request.js)",
    "Ajv schema validator — compiled via SchemaController (lib/schema-controller.js) for request validation (headers, params, querystring, body) and response serialization",
    "Pino logger (lib/logger-factory.js, lib/logger-pino.js) — all internal and request logging goes through Pino. Child loggers created per-request in routeHandler (lib/route.js:457-583)",
    "diagnostics_channel (Node.js built-in) — fastify.request.handler tracing channel used in preHandlerCallbackInner (lib/handle-request.js:156-199) for async request diagnostics",
    "light-my-request (for inject, fastify.js) — fake HTTP injection library used in testing and the .inject() method",
    "avvio — plugin boot system that orchestrates plugin registration, encapsulation, and the ready/close lifecycle (fastify.js imports Avvio)",
    "node:stream / node:stream/web — used in sendStream/sendWebStream (lib/reply.js) for piping stream and web-stream payloads to the HTTP response"
  ],
  "failure_points": [
    "Invalid URL → onBadUrl (fastify.js:549-580) returns 400 'FST_ERR_BAD_URL' or calls frameworkErrors callback",
    "Max param length exceeded → onMaxParamLength (fastify.js:582-613) returns 414 or calls frameworkErrors callback",
    "No matching route → defaultRoute (fastify.js:537-546) → fourOhFour.router.lookup → basic404 handler (lib/four-oh-four.js:52-60) returns 404 JSON",
    "Unrecognized Content-Type → handleRequest (lib/handle-request.js:21-72) returns 415 (FST_ERR_CTP_INVALID_MEDIA_TYPE)",
    "Schema validation failure → validationCompleted (lib/handle-request.js:56-68): if attachValidation=false, reply.send(validationErr) triggers error handler; if attachValidation=true, error stored in request.validationError and handler still runs",
    "Handler throws synchronously → preHandlerCallbackInner (lib/handle-request.js:156-199) catches err, sets reply[kReplyIsError]=true, calls reply.send(err) → onErrorHook → error handler chain",
    "Handler returns rejected promise → wrapThenable (lib/wrap-thenable.js) catches rejection, calls reply.send(err)",
    "Handler timeout → routeHandler (lib/route.js:457-583) sets AbortController timeout; if handler exceeds configured handlerTimeout, AbortController aborts and reply.send(FST_ERR_HANDLER_TIMEOUT) is called",
    "Schema compilation failure during route registration → route() function (lib/route.js:204-454) throws FST_ERR_SCH_VALIDATION_BUILD or FST_ERR_SCH_SERIALIZATION_BUILD before server starts",
    "Stream error during response → sendStream/sendWebStream (lib/reply.js:821-874, 748-819) log error via logStreamError, call onErrorHook for non-sent responses, or destroy socket for already-sent responses",
    "Server closing while request arrives → routeHandler (lib/route.js:476-487) returns 503 Service Unavailable immediately if return503OnClosing is true (default)",
    "Client disconnection before response → if context.onRequestAbort is set, onRequestAbortHookRunner (lib/hooks.js) runs abort hooks; otherwise handled silently by Node.js",
    "Client error on socket → server.on('clientError', defaultClientErrorHandler) (fastify.js:951-989) responds with 408 (timeout), 431 (header overflow), or 400 (general) then destroys socket",
    "Error inside error handler → handleError (lib/error-handler.js:30-76) chains error handlers via prototype chain; if all fail, fallbackErrorHandler writes a raw response",
    "onError hook throws → onSendHookRunner wraps it; handleError (lib/reply.js:onErrorHook) falls through to raw fallback response"
  ],
  "evidence": [
    "fastify.js:wrapRouting (lines 795-812) — preRouting callback, entry point from Node.js http.Server 'request' event",
    "lib/route.js:buildRouting (lines 77-584) — returns routing (router.lookup), route, routeHandler and lifecycle orchestration",
    "lib/route.js:routeHandler (lines 457-583) — creates Request/Reply, runs lifecycle hooks (onRequest → runPreParsing → handleRequest)",
    "lib/route.js:runPreParsing (lines 644-659) — runs preParsing hooks, transitions to handleRequest",
    "lib/handle-request.js:handleRequest (lines 21-72) — determines body parsing strategy, calls handler()",
    "lib/handle-request.js:handler (lines 26-33) — runs preValidation hooks then preValidationCallback",
    "lib/handle-request.js:preValidationCallback (lines 35-54) — runs schema validation",
    "lib/handle-request.js:validationCompleted (lines 56-68) — runs preHandler hooks",
    "lib/handle-request.js:preHandlerCallbackInner (lines 156-199) — executes user route handler, wraps thenable, catches errors",
    "lib/reply.js:Reply.prototype.send (lines 150-233) — dispatches payload to serialization/on-send/error paths",
    "lib/reply.js:onSendEnd (lines 585-691) — writes headers + body to Node.js response, handles streams/Buffers/strings/Web Streams/Response objects",
    "lib/reply.js:setupResponseListeners (lines 953-995) — listens for finish/error events, runs onResponse hooks, logs completion",
    "lib/hooks.js:onRequestHookRunner, preParsingHookRunner, preValidationHookRunner, preHandlerHookRunner, preSerializationHookRunner, onSendHookRunner, onResponseHookRunner — all hook runners generated by hookRunnerGenerator or specialized variants",
    "lib/error-handler.js:handleError (lines 30-76) — error handler chain execution",
    "lib/four-oh-four.js:fourOhFour (lines 32-191) — 404 handler system with encapsulated contexts",
    "lib/server.js:getServerInstance (lines 103-140) — creates http/https/http2 Server, wires httpHandler to 'request' event",
    "lib/server.js:createServer (lines 14-86) — returns {server, listen} and manages dual-stack binding for localhost",
    "lib/content-type-parser.js — pluggable body parsing per Content-Type"
  ],
  "summary": "The most representative user-facing workflow in Fastify is the HTTP request lifecycle: an incoming HTTP request is received by a Node.js server, routed by find-my-way (a radix-tree router) to a routeHandler, which creates Request and Reply objects and runs a well-defined lifecycle of 10 hook phases (onRequest → preParsing → body parsing → preValidation → schema validation → preHandler → handler execution → preSerialization → onSend → onResponse). The reply.send() method intelligently handles payloads of all types (JSON objects, strings, Buffers, streams, web streams, Response objects, null/undefined) into HTTP responses. Error handling flows through a prototype-chain-based error handler hierarchy with onError hooks, and there are robust safeguards at every stage: load shedding during shutdown (503), handler timeouts via AbortController, content-type validation (415), schema validation errors, stream error logging, client error handling (400/408/431), and a fallback 404 handler with encapsulation support."
}
```

Now I have traced the complete lifecycle. Here is the result:

```json
{
  "workflow_name": "Incoming HTTP Request Lifecycle",
  "flow": [
    "1. TCP connection accepted by Node.js http/https/http2 server → `lib/server.js:createServer` invokes `httpHandler` (the `preRouting` wrapper)",
    "2. URL rewrite check → `fastify.js:wrapRouting` (line ~790) checks `rewriteUrl` option, rewrites `req.url` if configured",
    "3. Router lookup → `lib/route.js:router.routing` (line ~65, bound `FindMyWay.lookup`) matches method+URL against the Radix tree, returns matched route context",
    "4. Route handler entry → `lib/route.js:routeHandler` (line ~500): creates `Request` (via `context.Request` constructor from `lib/request.js`) and `Reply` (via `context.Reply` constructor from `lib/reply.js`), logs `incomingRequest`, sets up handler timeout via `AbortController` + `setTimeout`, registers `setupResponseListeners` (reply finish/error listeners)",
    "5. onRequest hooks → `lib/route.js` line ~620: `onRequestHookRunner(context.onRequest, request, reply, runPreParsing)` runs user-registered onRequest hooks serially",
    "6. preParsing hooks → `lib/route.js:runPreParsing` line ~650: `preParsingHookRunner(context.preParsing, request, reply, ...)` allows payload transformation, then calls `handleRequest.call(request.server, null, request, reply)`",
    "7. Content-Type parsing & body dispatch → `lib/handle-request.js:handleRequest`: for bodyless methods (GET/HEAD/TRACE) calls `handler` directly; for body methods checks Content-Type header, runs the content-type parser via `request[kRouteContext].contentTypeParser.run(...)`, which streams the request body and calls back to `handler`",
    "8. preValidation hooks → `lib/handle-request.js:handler` → `preValidationHookRunner(context.preValidation, request, reply, preValidationCallback)`",
    "9. Schema validation → `lib/handle-request.js:preValidationCallback` → `validateSchema(reply[kRouteContext], request)` from `lib/validation.js:validate` — runs compiled AJV (or custom) validators for params, body, querystring, headers. Supports both sync and async validators",
    "10. preHandler hooks → `lib/handle-request.js:validationCompleted` → `preHandlerHookRunner(context.preHandler, request, reply, preHandlerCallback)`",
    "11. Route handler execution → `lib/handle-request.js:preHandlerCallbackInner` → `context.handler(request, reply)` — the user-provided handler function. Return values are sent via `reply.send(result)`. Thenable/promise return values are handled by `lib/wrap-thenable.js:wrapThenable`",
    "12. Reply.send → `lib/reply.js:Reply.prototype.send`: inspects payload type (Error→onError, stream/Response→direct send, undefined→onSend, object→serialize), sets Content-Type header, calls preSerialization→onSend chain",
    "13. preSerialization hooks → `lib/reply.js:preSerializationHook` → `preSerializationHookRunner(context.preSerialization, request, reply, payload, ...)` allows payload modification before serialization",
    "14. Serialize payload → `lib/reply.js:serialize` → `getSchemaSerializer(context, statusCode, contentType)` uses pre-compiled fast-json-stringify serializer for schema'd responses, otherwise `JSON.stringify`",
    "15. onSend hooks → `lib/reply.js:onSendHook` → `onSendHookRunner(context.onSend, request, reply, payload, wrapOnSendEnd)` allows final payload modification",
    "16. Write response → `lib/reply.js:onSendEnd`: calls `safeWriteHead(statusCode, headers)`, then writes payload via `res.end()`, or pipes stream via `sendStream`/`sendWebStream`, or sends chunked data for HTTP/2 large payloads via `writeHttp2Payload`. Sets Content-Length for non-stream payloads",
    "17. onResponse hooks → `lib/reply.js:setupResponseListeners` callback → `onResponseHookRunner(context.onResponse, request, reply, onResponseCallback)` runs after response 'finish' event",
    "18. Response logging → `lib/reply.js:onResponseCallback` → `reply.server[kLogController].requestCompleted(err, request, reply)` finalizes request logging"
  ],
  "state_transitions": [
    "`kState.started=false` → `avvio.on('start')` sets `kState.started=true` (fastify.js:373)",
    "`kState.ready=false` → after all plugins boot and `onReady` hooks run, `kState.ready=true`, `kState.booting=false`, `readyResolver=null` (fastify.js:559-562)",
    "`kState.listening=false` → server.listen() callback sets `kState.listening=true` (lib/server.js, listenCallback + listenPromise)",
    "`kState.closing=false` → `avvio.once('preReady')` registers onClose handler that sets `kState.closing=true` (fastify.js:380-381)",
    "Per-request: Request object created → `reply[kReplyIsError]=false` → hooks execute → if hook err: `reply[kReplyIsError]=true`, `reply.send(err)` → if validation fails: `reply.send(validationErr)` → handler runs → `reply.send(result)` → onSend → `res.writeHead`/`res.end` → 'finish' event → onResponse hooks → `requestCompleted` log"
  ],
  "external_boundaries": [
    "Node.js HTTP/HTTPS/HTTP2 server (`lib/server.js:createServer`) — accepts TCP connections, emits `request` event calling the `httpHandler`",
    "FindMyWay router (`lib/route.js:buildRouting` wraps `find-my-way`) — URL/method matching against registered routes",
    "`diagnostics_channel` (`fastify.request.handler` tracing channel in `lib/handle-request.js` and `lib/wrap-thenable.js`) — publishes start/asyncStart/asyncEnd/end/error events for APM/tracing",
    "`diagnostics_channel` `fastify.initialization` channel (`fastify.js:451`) — publishes on instance creation",
    "Logging system (`lib/logger-factory.js`, `lib/log-controller.js`) — pluggable via Pino or custom logger, called at request inbound, outbound, errors, and stream errors",
    "Content-type parser system (`lib/content-type-parser.js`) — delegates body parsing to registered parsers (default JSON, text, or custom via `addContentTypeParser`)",
    "Validator/serializer compilers (`@fastify/ajv-compiler`, `@fastify/fast-json-stringify-compiler`) — AJV for input validation, fast-json-stringify for output serialization",
    "`light-my-request` for `inject()` testing utility (`fastify.js:inject`) — simulates HTTP requests without a server"
  ],
  "failure_points": [
    "Bad URL / max param length → `fastify.js:onBadUrl` (400) / `onMaxParamLength` (414), uses `frameworkErrors` callback if set",
    "Route not found → `fastify.js:defaultRoute` delegates to `fourOhFour.router.lookup(req, res)` — 404 via `lib/four-oh-four.js` with customizable `setNotFoundHandler`",
    "Invalid Content-Type → `lib/handle-request.js` line 70: `FST_ERR_CTP_INVALID_MEDIA_TYPE` → `reply.status(415).send(...)`",
    "Schema validation failure → `lib/validation.js:wrapValidationError` → `schemaErrorFormatter(errors, dataVar)` returns error with 400 status",
    "Handler timeout → `lib/route.js:routeHandler` (lines ~530-540) AbortController + setTimeout → `FST_ERR_HANDLER_TIMEOUT` → `reply.send(err)`",
    "Handler throws → `lib/handle-request.js:preHandlerCallbackInner` catch → `reply[kReplyIsError]=true; reply.send(err)`",
    "Error during error handling → `lib/error-handler.js:handleError` → chains to `fallbackErrorHandler` → `serializeError`",
    "Serialization failure → `lib/reply.js:wrapSerializationError` → `onErrorHook(reply, e)`",
    "Reply already sent → `lib/reply.js:Reply.prototype.send` → `FST_ERR_REP_ALREADY_SENT` logged as warning",
    "Server closing during request → `lib/route.js:routeHandler` closing check (line ~510) returns 503 'Service Unavailable' when `return503OnClosing=true`",
    "Socket/stream errors → `lib/reply.js:sendStream`/`sendWebStream` eos handlers → `logStreamError` + destroy, or `onErrorHook` if headers not yet sent",
    "Client abort → `lib/route.js:routeHandler` onRequestAbort hook runner via `req.on('close', ...)` when `req.aborted`"
  ],
  "evidence": [
    "`fastify.js` line 90-461 — Instance creation, Avvio bootstrapping, `wrapRouting` pre-routing function, `defaultRoute`, `ready` state machine",
    "`fastify.js` line 788-802 — `wrapRouting` defines the `preRouting` function (URL rewrite + router dispatch) that wraps the HTTP handler",
    "`lib/server.js:createServer` (line 25-122) — Creates http/https/http2 server, manages `listen`, multiple bindings, forceCloseConnections",
    "`lib/route.js:routeHandler` (line ~500-640) — Central request handler: creates Request/Reply, sets up timeouts, runs onRequest/preParsing hooks, manages closing state",
    "`lib/route.js:runPreParsing` (line ~650) — Transitions from route handler into handle-request dispatch",
    "`lib/handle-request.js:handleRequest` (line 23-82) — Content-type detection, bodyless vs bodywith method dispatch, 415 handling",
    "`lib/handle-request.js:handler` (line 84-95) — Runs preValidation hooks",
    "`lib/handle-request.js:preValidationCallback` (line 97-114) — Runs `validateSchema`, then dispatches to preHandler",
    "`lib/handle-request.js:preHandlerCallbackInner` (line 142-185) — Executes `context.handler`, handles sync return values and thenable/promise via `wrapThenable`",
    "`lib/validation.js:validate` (line ~70-130) — Runs compiled AJV validators for params, body, querystring, headers; supports async validators",
    "`lib/reply.js:Reply.prototype.send` (line 115-200) — Inspects payload type, sets Content-Type, dispatches to preSerialization→onSend→write",
    "`lib/reply.js:onSendEnd` (line ~310-390) — Writes HTTP status code, headers, and body (string/buffer/stream/webstream)",
    "`lib/reply.js:setupResponseListeners` (line ~490-530) — Hooks finish/error events on raw response, runs onResponse hooks",
    "`lib/hooks.js:onSendHookRunner`, `preValidationHookRunner`, `preHandlerHookRunner`, `preSerializationHookRunner`, `onResponseHookRunner` (lines ~125-230) — Hook iteration engines with error propagation",
    "`lib/error-handler.js:handleError` (line ~30-80) — Error handler chain traversal, fallback serialization",
    "`lib/wrap-thenable.js:wrapThenable` (line 14-55) — Promise resolution for async handlers, publishes diagnostics channel events",
    "`lib/route.js` line 530-540 — Handler timeout via AbortController + setTimeout"
  ],
  "summary": "The HTTP request lifecycle is a linear pipeline through Fastify's core: (1) a TCP connection hits Node's HTTP server (`lib/server.js`); (2) `wrapRouting` applies optional URL rewriting then dispatches to FindMyWay for route matching (`lib/route.js`); (3) `routeHandler` creates `Request`/`Reply` objects, sets up handler timeout and response-finish listeners, then runs lifecycle hooks in order — onRequest → preParsing → parse body via content-type parser → preValidation → schema validation (AJV) → preHandler → the user's handler function (`context.handler`); (4) the handler's return value flows through `reply.send`, which runs preSerialization hooks, serializes via fast-json-stringify (if schema'd) or JSON.stringify, then runs onSend hooks before writing the HTTP response; (5) after the response finishes, onResponse hooks run and request logging completes. Errors at any stage short-circuit through the error-handler chain (custom per-route or default), which falls back to a safe error serialization. The entire pipeline is observable via the `fastify.request.handler` diagnostics tracing channel and the pluggable logger."
}
```
